assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading the control plane first before any node pools
assertions.reason: Response clearly shows upgrading control plane in Phase 1 before any node pools in Phase 2

assertions.expectation: Provides a specific node pool upgrade order with rationale (coordinating→data→master, or similar logical sequence)
assertions.reason: Provides specific order: coordinating → master → data nodes, with rationale that coordinating nodes have minimal impact

assertions.expectation: Addresses PV safety: regional pd-ssd volumes can reattach to nodes in other zones, which is good for upgrades
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Regional pd-ssd volumes will automatically reattach to new nodes' in safety considerations

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting PDB for Elasticsearch master nodes to ensure quorum (minAvailable=2 for 3-master setup)
assertions.reason: Does not mention Pod Disruption Budgets (PDB) at all in the response

assertions.expectation: Recommends maxSurge=1, maxUnavailable=0 for all ES node pools (conservative, data-safe)
assertions.reason: Explicitly shows --max-surge=1 and --max-unavailable=0 commands for both master and data node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Advises verifying Elasticsearch cluster health (green status) between each node pool upgrade
assertions.reason: Shows monitoring scripts and curl commands to check cluster health status between upgrades, specifically waiting for green status

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking PV reclaim policy is set to Retain (not Delete) as a safety measure
assertions.reason: Does not mention checking or setting PV reclaim policy anywhere in the response

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for checking StatefulSet rollout status
assertions.reason: Shows kubectl rollout restart command but does not include commands for checking rollout status like kubectl rollout status

assertions.expectation: Recommends taking an Elasticsearch snapshot backup before starting
assertions.reason: First step in pre-upgrade preparation explicitly shows creating an Elasticsearch snapshot with curl command
